<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal `O` is at row 04 column D, but the direct path to the right at row 02 column C is lava, so I should not move forward. To go around safely, I need to face down toward row 03 column B, which is a safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column B and I am facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D, and there is a safe path downward into row 03 column B, then toward the right. Moving forward from my current position goes to row 03 column B, which is safe. 
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>
FRENTE

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B facing down (`v`). The goal `O` is at row 04 column D, but the cell directly ahead at row 05 column B is a wall, so I cannot move forward. To head toward the goal safely, I should turn left to face right (`>`), which will let me move along row 04 toward column D.  
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B, facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D, but the direct path through column C is lava (`L`), so I cannot move forward. I should turn to face up or down to find a safe route, but both adjacent cells above and below in column B are floors? Actually above row 03 column B is safe, so moving upward is the safest next step. From facing right, turning counter-clockwise will face up.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO

<thought>

</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
